prerequisites: An Avni account with access to a hosted Avni server (for example app.avniproject.org) or a self-hosted Avni deployment.
A user account created in the Avni Admin web app and assigned the "User" role so it can call the API.
Log in once through the Avni web application with the account so it is not prompted to change its password on first API login.
The username and password for that Avni account.
A start_date marking the earliest point from which to sync data.

authMethods.name: Username and password (AWS Cognito token)
authMethods.summary: Avni authenticates through AWS Cognito. Provide the account username and password; these are used to obtain an auth token that is sent with each API request via the auth-token header. The connector maps to the username config field and the password secret field.

setupSteps.title: Create an API user in the Avni Admin web app
setupSteps.body: In the Avni Admin web app, create a user account that will be used by the connector and assign it the "User" role so it is permitted to call the Avni API.

setupSteps.title: Log in once via the web application
setupSteps.body: Sign in to the Avni web app with the new account at least once. This clears the forced first-login password change so the credentials work directly when authenticating through the API.

setupSteps.title: Provide username and password
setupSteps.body: Enter the account's username in the username field and its password in the password field. Avni uses these credentials to authenticate via AWS Cognito and obtain the auth token used for API calls.

setupSteps.title: Set the start date
setupSteps.body: Set start_date to the earliest date from which the connector should begin syncing records.

sources.title: Avni API Guide - Introduction (authentication, user role, token)
Avni Field Service and Data Collection Platform
